matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 2 > Lunch > Japanese Teriyaki Salmon Donburi

node.name: Japanese Teriyaki Salmon Donburi
node.short_description: Pan-seared salmon fillet glazed with homemade teriyaki sauce, served over a bowl of steamed rice with steamed broccoli and pickled ginger.
node.meal_suggestion: Pan-sear salmon skin-side down until crispy, then glaze with a mix of soy sauce, mirin, and a touch of sugar. Serve over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and a small mound of pickled ginger. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced nori.
node.nutrition_note: Salmon is rich in omega-3 fatty acids supporting heart and brain health; broccoli provides fiber, vitamin C, and sulforaphane; rice offers sustained energy. Teriyaki glaze contains sodium and sugar — make homemade to control amounts. Pickled ginger aids digestion.

context_trace.description: Matched atom(content =~ "teriyaki salmon bowl") on Japanese Teriyaki Salmon Donburi (Score: 0.51)

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 6 > Lunch > Salmon Salad Wrap — Garden Veg Bake

node.name: Salmon Salad Wrap — Garden Veg Bake
node.short_description: Protein-packed salmon salad with yogurt dressing, celery and greens wrapped in a whole-wheat tortilla for a balanced lunch.
node.meal_suggestion: Serve as a cold wrap for portability; for the Garden Veg Bake version, bake the salmon-yogurt mix with roasted vegetables briefly, then wrap or serve over greens.
node.nutrition_note: Protein-rich (canned salmon, Greek yogurt) with omega-3s; fiber from greens and celery; whole-wheat tortilla adds complex carbs; contains dairy; watch sodium from canned salmon.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 2 > Lunch > Korean Bibimbap Bowl

node.name: Korean Bibimbap Bowl
node.short_description: A colorful Korean rice bowl topped with sautéed vegetables, seasoned beef, a fried egg, and spicy gochujang sauce — balanced and vibrant.
node.meal_suggestion: Arrange sautéed spinach, bean sprouts, carrots, and zucchini over steamed rice. Top with seasoned ground beef and a sunny-side-up egg. Serve with gochujang (Korean chili paste) mixed with sesame oil on the side. Mix everything together before eating for the best flavor.
node.nutrition_note: A well-rounded meal with protein from beef and egg, fiber and vitamins from assorted vegetables, and energy from rice. Gochujang contains capsaicin which may aid metabolism. Moderate in calories; control portions of rice and gochujang to manage carbs and sodium.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 3 > Lunch > Bean Burrito Bowl — Maple-Kissed Set

node.name: Bean Burrito Bowl — Maple-Kissed Set
node.short_description: Bean Burrito Bowl with black beans, rice, corn, lettuce, salsa, and cheddar.
node.meal_suggestion: For a lighter option, use reduced-fat cheddar or dairy-free cheese; swap white rice for brown rice to boost fiber; add avocado or extra veggies for healthy fats and micronutrients.
node.nutrition_note: Balanced vegetarian bowl with plant protein from black beans and fiber from beans and rice; corn and salsa add micronutrients, while cheddar provides calcium and some saturated fat.

matched_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Mother > Day 1 > Lunch > Soba Noodle Bowl — Mild-Spice Style

node.name: Soba Noodle Bowl — Mild-Spice Style
node.short_description: Soba noodles with cucumber, carrots, and egg in a mild-spice sesame dressing.
node.meal_suggestion: Great as a light lunch. To boost protein, add tofu or grilled chicken; add greens like spinach or bok choy; reduce sodium by using a low-sodium sesame dressing or serving the dressing on the side.
node.nutrition_note: Balanced bowl with complex carbs from soba, fiber from cucumber and carrots, and protein from egg. Contains sesame and egg; gluten content depends on soba (many varieties contain wheat). Sodium can be moderate to high from the dressing. Mild spice; avoid if sesame or egg allergies.
